name: kedi-vscode
description: LSP-powered language support for Kedi DSL: highlighting via semantic tokens (tree-sitter on the server), diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition, references, rename, outline, signature help, inlay hints, formatting, plus Python LSP feature forwarding inside fenced ```python``` blocks.

kedi.lsp.usePythonExtension.description: Spawn the kedi-lsp server with the Python interpreter selected by the Microsoft Python extension (env-aware: venv, conda, pyenv, poetry, uv, etc.). When the interpreter changes in the IDE, the server is restarted automatically.

kedi.lsp.pythonPath.description: Explicit Python interpreter path to use when kedi.lsp.usePythonExtension is false or the Python extension is unavailable. The server is launched as `<pythonPath> -m kedi.lsp.server`.

kedi.lsp.serverCommand.description: Final fallback command spawned over stdio when no Python interpreter could be resolved (must be on PATH).

kedi.lsp.trace.server.description: Trace level for the LSP client/server channel (visible in the 'Kedi Language Server' output panel).

kedi.embeddedPython.enable.description: Forward Hover / Go-to-Definition / Find References to the Python language server (Pylance/Pyright) when the cursor is inside a fenced ```python``` block or a backtick `python_expr` region.
